This action rescinded the 2020 rule that never went into effect [1].

2.  **Proposed Removal of Discriminatory Effects Regulations:** HUD has proposed a rule to remove its regulations concerning discriminatory effects (disparate impact). This proposal would leave questions of disparate impact liability under the Fair Housing Act to the courts, removing the presumption of unlawful discrimination based solely on outcome variances among protected classes [2].

This proposal builds on the 2015 AFFH rule and would require certain recipients of HUD funds to proactively take meaningful actions to overcome segregation, promote fair housing choice, eliminate disparities, and foster inclusive communities.
